description:
# fz-RED: Reduce-at-callsite, mint at boundary

See `docs/bodies-are-boundaries.md` for the full design.

## The thing in one sentence

Replace the current "mint a spec per distinct callsite Descr tuple"
discovery loop with a **Module → Module reducer pass** that walks
from program roots (`main`, spawned fns), reduces each callsite
where possible, and leaves a call in place only at opacity
boundaries. The typer then types the *reduced* call graph, and the
spec hot mess disappears as a side effect.

## Concrete acceptance

- `ast_eval` expected.clif drops from ~1400 lines to under 100.
- `ast_eval` spec count drops from 50+ to ≤ 3.
- Every existing fixture passes three-path parity (interp + JIT + AOT).
- `fz dump --emit bodies` correctly attributes each body to a
  source-visible opacity boundary.
- Code that becomes dead is **deleted**, not stubbed.

## What this is NOT

- **Part 2 (allocations / reuse / DPS)** is `fz-q9g`, in flight
  separately. This epic does not touch allocation strategy.
- **Typed receive / send-site mailbox check** is gated on `fz-ul4.19`.
  Not in this epic.
- **Cross-module inlining** (`fz-ul4.11.27`) is orthogonal.

## Removals are first-class tickets

`fz-RED.8a/.8b/.8c` are explicit deletion tickets. The arc only
finishes when the now-dead machinery is gone.

## DAG

```
RED.0 SPIKE
   │
   ▼
RED.1 pattern reduction primitive
   │
   ▼  (also requires fz-ul4.43 pattern matrix landing)
RED.2 clause dispatch
   │
   ▼
RED.3 reducer scaffold (single-clause)
   │
   ▼
RED.4 recursive reduction with unroll budget
   │       └─ subsumes fz-ul4.11.28
   ▼
RED.5 closure_lit reduction
   │
   ▼
RED.6 re-bless every fixture golden
   │
   ├──► RED.7 --explain-bodies diagnostic
   ├──► RED.9 @spec as narrowing contract
   └──► RED.8a REMOVE per-callsite spec minting
          │
          ▼
        RED.8b REMOVE closure_lit return chain
          │
          ▼
        RED.8c REMOVE ir_inline as separate pass
```
title: Reduce-at-callsite, mint at boundary
